# FEAT061 — Code Review

## Verdict

**APPROVED.** A clean, minimal, contract-fixing change. The dispatcher
now forwards `state` into the handler ctx (one line), the
`ToolHandler` ctx is typed as a named shape (one line), and the four
migrated handlers replace the defensive cast with typed access (one
line each). Four new dispatcher-level regression tests prove the
forwarding contract end-to-end, one per migrated skill, all calling
`dispatchSkill` (not the handler directly) per architect's binding
condition 4.

No fixes required by the reviewer. All 7 design-review conditions are
satisfied. Gates pass clean (377 tests, `tsc --noEmit` only the
pre-existing `executor.ts:229`, `npm run build:web` exports). One
latent executor bug surfaced during review (notes drop on
`applyWrites`) — out of scope per condition 7, flagged below for a
follow-up FEAT.

## Files reviewed

**Source (3 + 4 = 7):**

- `src/types/skills.ts` — `ToolHandler` ctx typed as named shape `{ phrase: string; skillId: string; state?: unknown }` (was `Record<string, unknown>`). NO `AppState` import added.
- `src/modules/skillDispatcher.ts` — line 133 forwards `state: options.state` to handler ctx.
- `src/skills/task_management/handlers.ts:38` — cast removal.
- `src/skills/notes_capture/handlers.ts:28` — cast removal.
- `src/skills/calendar_management/handlers.ts:43` — cast removal.
- `src/skills/inbox_triage/handlers.ts:55` — cast removal.

**Tests (4):**

- `src/modules/task_management.test.ts` — +1 dispatcher-level regression test, `setDataRoot(os.tmpdir())` redirect.
- `src/modules/notes_capture.test.ts` — +1 regression test, `setDataRoot` redirect, post-flush signal assertion.
- `src/modules/calendar_management.test.ts` — +1 regression test, `setDataRoot` redirect, `contradictionIndex` added to fixture state.
- `src/modules/inbox_triage.test.ts` — +1 regression test, `setDataRoot` redirect, three-collection assertion (tasks/calendar/notes).

## §6 conditions audit

| # | Condition | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Dispatcher line 133 forwards state | Y — `git diff` shows `state: options.state` added to handler ctx literal at line 133. No other dispatcher changes. |
| 2 | `ToolHandler` ctx is named-shape, `state?: unknown`, no `AppState` import | Y — `src/types/skills.ts` line 27 reads `ctx: { phrase: string; skillId: string; state?: unknown }`. Grep for `AppState` in `src/types/skills.ts` returns no matches. |
| 3 | Four migrated handlers replace the cast with `ctx.state as AppState \| undefined` | Y — verified all four diffs: `(ctx as { state?: AppState }).state` → `ctx.state as AppState \| undefined`. Behavior-identical: same runtime value, same compile-time narrow. |
| 4 | One Story-2 regression test per migrated skill, calling `dispatchSkill` (NOT the handler directly) | Y — verified by reading each test diff. All four invoke `dispatchSkill(makeRoute(<id>), <phrase>, { registry, enabledSkillIds, state, llmClient })`. None bypass the dispatcher. |
| 5 | `tsc --noEmit` clean (only pre-existing `executor.ts:229`) | Y — re-ran post-review, single error `executor.ts(229,58): error TS2339: Property 'length' does not exist on type '{}'`, identical to baseline. |
| 6 | `npm run build:web` exports | Y — re-ran post-review, exports 7 bundles + 9 files cleanly. |
| 7 | No changes to `chat.tsx`, `inbox.ts`, `executor.ts`, `assembler.ts`, `router.ts`, `types/index.ts`, or any other dispatcher caller | Y — `git diff` against each file is empty. Only the seven files declared in scope changed (plus `_manifest.json`, regenerated by the feature-kit CLI). |

All seven conditions: **PASS**.

## Code observations

**1. Cast-removal behavior equivalence.** Both forms — old
`(ctx as { state?: AppState }).state` and new
`ctx.state as AppState | undefined` — produce identical runtime values
(reading the `state` property off the ctx object) and identical
compile-time narrowing (`AppState | undefined`). The only difference
is *where* the narrowing happens: the old form casts the whole ctx to
a sub-shape, the new form casts the already-typed `ctx.state` field.
No behavior change. Verified by reading all four handler diffs.

**2. Architect's "processBundle bypasses state forwarding" claim
needs a footnote.** The architect's design review §4 says the inbox
timer path (`processBundle`) "deliberately does not pass `state` to
`dispatchSkill`." Reading `inbox.ts:98` directly: it does in fact
pass `state` (`dispatchSkill(route, chunk, { state })`). Pre-FEAT061,
the dispatcher dropped `state`, so the handler's `applyWrites` block
never ran on the timer path either — `processBundle` claimed
"Handler already called applyWrites" at line 128 but that was false.
Post-FEAT061, both the chat and timer paths now correctly run
`applyWrites` exactly once (in the handler), and `processBundle`'s
post-write derived-state refresh runs as designed. **This is the
correct outcome** — FEAT061 actually completes the contract for both
paths, not just the chat path. The architect's mental model was
slightly off but the fix lands correctly. Worth noting for future
reviewers: the timer path was equally broken pre-FEAT061; the silent
data-loss footprint was wider than the spec described.

**3. The four Story-2 tests are the right shape.** Each test:

- Builds a fixture state with the relevant collection empty.
- Stubs the LLM client to return one canonical write.
- Loads the production registry via `loadProductionRegistry()`.
- Invokes `dispatchSkill(...)` with `enabledSkillIds` set.
- Asserts the relevant fixture-state collection mutated.

This is exactly the contract the architect specified in condition 4
and design review §8. None of the four bypasses the dispatcher.

**4. `setDataRoot(os.tmpdir())` mitigates the FEAT060 leakage
pattern.** All four test files now redirect filesystem writes to a
unique temp dir at module load time (before any test runs). Verified
post-test-run that `git status` shows no fixture leakage in the repo
root.

**5. notes_capture / inbox_triage post-flush signal assertion is
sufficient.** Both tests use `"notes" in state._loadedCounts` as the
post-flush witness instead of asserting `state.notes.notes.length`
grew. This is correct given the latent executor bug (see below).
The `_loadedCounts` key is populated by `applyWrites → flush()`, so
its presence proves the dispatcher forwarded state AND the flush ran.
That's the contract FEAT061 is actually enforcing — anything beyond
that (whether the inner array got the row appended) is the executor's
concern, not the dispatcher's.

**6. inbox_triage timer path stays state-preserving but applyWrites
behavior changed correctly.** Architect's callout 3 was that
`processBundle` should not change. Verified: `git diff src/modules/inbox.ts`
is empty. The behavior change is hidden inside the dispatcher → handler
contract, where it belongs.

**7. No real user data in the new test fixtures.** Phrases used:
"call the dentist", "save this idea: review the architecture diagram",
"schedule a sync on Friday at 3pm", "Task A by Friday. Meeting Tue 3pm.
Idea: Project X kickoff doc." All generic placeholders. Nothing tied
to the user's real life.

**8. No new top-level Node imports in source files (FEAT054 B5
compliance).** The test files import `fs`, `os`, `path` at top level
— that's fine because tests are Node-only. The four handler source
files and the dispatcher / types files added zero new imports.

## Latent bug investigation

**Confirmed real:** `executor.ts:applyAdd` does NOT append to
`notes.notes` on `add` writes for the `notes` file key.

**Evidence.** Read `src/modules/executor.ts:590-601`:

```ts
const target = (state as any)[fileKey];
for (const listKey of ["tasks", "events", "items", "suggestions"] as const) {
  if (Array.isArray(target?.[listKey])) {
    if (target[listKey].length >= MAX_ARRAY_ITEMS) { ... }
    target[listKey].push(d);
    return;
  }
}
Object.assign(target, d);
```

`NotesFile` (per `src/types/index.ts:234-237`) is shaped
`{ _summary: string; notes: Note[] }` — the inner array key is
`notes`, which is **not in the loop's list**. When the executor
processes a `{ file: "notes", action: "add" }` write, the for-loop
falls through and `Object.assign(target, d)` runs instead, which
silently overwrites `_summary` / `notes` fields on the
`NotesFile` shape with the per-row data, NOT appending to the
`notes` array.

**Impact post-FEAT061.** With FEAT061 landed, the chat-driven
notes_capture and inbox_triage paths correctly forward state to the
handler, the handler correctly calls `applyWrites`, but the executor
silently drops the note row instead of appending it. The user's chat
phrase "save this idea: …" succeeds at the dispatcher / handler /
applyWrites layer but the note never lands in `notes.notes`.

**Why not fix here.** Out of scope per design-review condition 7
("No changes to executor.ts"). Architect explicitly excluded it. The
spec for FEAT061 is the dispatcher / type / cast-cleanup. Adding a
case to `applyAdd`'s array-loop list is a different fix with its own
risk surface (array-cap semantics, dedup interactions, the `notes`
file's `_loadedCounts` baseline behavior — all need their own design
review).

**Why the FEAT061 tests still ship green.** notes_capture and
inbox_triage assert `"notes" in state._loadedCounts` instead of
`state.notes.notes.length === 1`. That's the post-flush signal — it
proves the dispatcher forwarded state and `applyWrites → flush` ran
all the way through, even if the inner write was silently dropped.
That's exactly what FEAT061 is contractually responsible for. The
note-append failure is the executor's contract.

**Recommendation.** File a follow-up FEAT (suggested **FEAT062**:
"Fix executor `applyAdd` array-loop to include `notes`"). One-line
change to add `"notes"` to the list at `executor.ts:591`, plus
matching test that asserts `state.notes.notes.length` grew after a
notes `add`. The notes_capture and inbox_triage Story-2 tests should
be tightened from the `_loadedCounts` signal to the array-length
assertion as part of that FEAT.

## Things NOT in scope

- **The executor latent bug for `notes`.** Out of scope per design
  review condition 7. Document above; follow-up FEAT recommended.
- **AGENTS.md / `docs/new_architecture_typescript.md` updates.**
  Tracked separately on the FEAT057-060 docs cleanup backlog. Not
  blocking.
- **Tightening `processBundle`'s claim that "Handler already called
  applyWrites" (the comment was true post-FEAT061, false pre-FEAT061
  — see Code observation 2).** Documentation polish; outside this
  FEAT's contract.
- **Required `state` on `ToolHandler` ctx.** Architect explicitly
  chose optional in §3.2. Direct-handler unit tests would otherwise
  need a fixture state argument, which is out of scope.
- **Adding new resolver keys to `SUPPORTED_KEYS`.** No new context
  keys needed by this fix.

## Sign-off

Code Reviewer approves. All 7 design-review conditions PASS. No
fixes applied (none required). Gates rerun post-review:

- `npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json` — only the pre-existing
  `executor.ts:229` warning. Clean.
- `npm run build:web` — exports cleanly.
- `node scripts/run-tests.js` — 377/377 passed across 16 suites.
- `git status` — no fixture leakage in repo root.

Status advanced to **Code Reviewed**. Tester picks up next.

## For the tester

Focus areas, in priority order:

1. **End-to-end chat-driven write smoke.** With the fix in place, a
   chat phrase that routes to each of the four migrated skills
   (`task_management`, `notes_capture`, `calendar_management`,
   `inbox_triage`) should now persist the create. Verify each one
   end-to-end on the running app (not just unit tests). The
   `notes_capture` chat path will surface the latent executor bug —
   the user-visible symptom is "I saved that for you" but the note
   doesn't appear in the notes UI. Document the latent bug in
   `FEAT061_test-results.md` with a recommended FEAT062 spec.

2. **Inbox timer path regression check.** The architect's design
   review claimed `processBundle` doesn't pass state, but in fact
   it does. Post-FEAT061 the handler's `applyWrites` runs on the
   timer path too. Verify (a) timer-driven inbox bundles still
   succeed end-to-end, (b) writes are not duplicated (handler
   `applyWrites` runs once, `processBundle` does NOT call
   `applyWrites` itself in v4 mode — only the legacy fallback
   does), (c) the inbox file is cleared on success. This was the
   path most at risk per the architect's §5 risk row 1.

3. **Calendar recurring-rejection prompt.** That path
   short-circuits before `applyWrites` (handler returns the
   clarification reply directly). Verify it still surfaces the
   "needs clarification" prompt correctly post-FEAT061 — the cast
   removal could in theory affect early-return narrowing, even
   though it's behavior-identical at runtime.

4. **Direct-handler unit tests still pass.** The four pre-existing
   direct-handler tests (which pass `state` directly into ctx) are
   unchanged by FEAT061. They should all still pass and prove the
   handler logic itself is correct given state. The new tests prove
   the dispatcher delivers state.

5. **No fixture leakage on repeated runs.** Run the test suite
   3-5 times in succession. Verify `git status` stays clean
   (`setDataRoot(os.tmpdir())` should redirect every flush).

6. **Disable test for v4 fallback.** `setV4SkillsEnabled` excluding
   each skill should still fall back to legacy correctly. The fix
   is on the v4 path only; the legacy path is unaffected.
